title: Dotfile.yaml
description: Schema for dots package manifest files (Dotfile.yaml)

requires.description: Other packages this package depends on. Each entry is a '<tap>/<package>' ref, or '@self/<package>' to resolve to the current package's tap (portable intra-tap references).

platforms.description: Restrict installation to these platforms (e.g. darwin, linux, darwin-arm64)

merge.description: Files to merge from multiple packages

platform.description: Platform-specific overrides keyed by OS or OS-arch

link_strategy.description: How files are linked to their targets

links.description: Map of source file paths to target paths. Targets support @alias paths: the default family (@home, @config, @data, @cache, @state, @bin); the XDG family (@xdg-config, @xdg-data, @xdg-cache, @xdg-state) which always resolves to XDG paths regardless of OS; and the Apple family (@apple-config, @apple-data, @apple-cache, @apple-logs, @apple-launchagents) which resolves to ~/Library subdirectories on darwin and errors on other platforms.

hooks.description: Lifecycle hook scripts or inline commands

pre_install.description: Script path or inline command run before installation

post_install.description: Script path or inline command run after installation

pre_remove.description: Script path or inline command run before removal

post_remove.description: Script path or inline command run after removal

pre_upgrade.description: Script path or inline command run before upgrade

post_upgrade.description: Script path or inline command run after upgrade

overlay.description: Declares this package as a layer on top of a base package

base.description: Base package reference ('<tap>/<package>' or '@self/<package>' to resolve to the current package's tap)

strategy.description: How overlay content is combined with the base

priority.description: Overlay priority (0-99, higher wins for replace)
